item.text:
[demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-nohook/demo/rate_limiter.py:13) already has the requested pruning:

```python
self.calls = [t for t in self.calls if now - t < self.window]
```

That drops timestamps older than the active window before checking `len(self.calls)`, so there was no file change needed from the state I can see.

--- Learning check ---

In this rate limiter, why does pruning need to happen before `len(self.calls) < self.limit` instead of after the limit check?
